title: Market Wrap: Sensex drops 516 points, Nifty closes below 24,200 amid fresh Iran-US escalations, smallcaps - The Economic Times
summary: Indian equities, including the Nifty 50 and Sensex, experienced significant declines on May 8th, with the Sensex dropping over 500 points and Nifty closing below 24,200, primarily due to escalating Iran-US tensions. Globally, markets showed mixed sentiment, with crude oil prices rising, impacting overall investor confidence.

title: Crude above USD 100, global tensions drag Sensex and Nifty lower - ChiniMandi
summary: Rising global tensions, particularly involving Iran-US, have pushed crude oil prices above $100 per barrel. This geopolitical instability significantly contributed to the downturn of Indian benchmark indices, with both Sensex and Nifty recording losses.

title: Nifty 50, Sensex prediction today: Check how Indian stock market is expected to trade on 8 May - Mint
summary: Analysts provided their outlook for the Indian stock market on May 8th, offering predictions for the Nifty 50 and Sensex. These forecasts help investors anticipate market movements and strategize their trading decisions for the day.

title: Stock market update: Sensex, Nifty end the week in red; Swiggy fails to deliver profit but narrows losses - theweek.in
summary: Indian benchmark indices, Sensex and Nifty, concluded the week in negative territory, reflecting a broader market downturn. Separately, Swiggy reported narrowing its losses, although it still failed to achieve profitability.

title: The world outlook 2026 – never a dull moment - flow – Deutsche Bank
summary: Deutsche Bank's 2026 world outlook anticipates a dynamic and eventful year for global markets and economies. The report suggests that investors should prepare for continuous shifts and challenges across various sectors.

title: Macro Observer: Kevin Warsh Says Shrinking the Federal Reserve's Balance Sheet Is No Easy Task - Bitget
summary: Former Federal Reserve Governor Kevin Warsh highlighted the significant challenges involved in reducing the Federal Reserve's balance sheet. This process is crucial for monetary policy normalization but presents complexities for the broader economy.

title: Citi appoints Raj Rathi as head of mergers & acquisitions in India - Mint
summary: Citi has named Raj Rathi as its new head of mergers and acquisitions for India. This appointment signifies a strategic move by the bank to strengthen its M&A operations within the growing Indian market.

title: US M&A activity insights: March 2026 - EY
summary: EY has released its insights into M&A activity within the United States for March 2026. The report provides a detailed analysis of recent trends and deal volumes in the American market.

title: The global M&A boom is rolling into 2026 as AI sparks deal frenzy — but cash is getting tight - CNBC
summary: The global mergers and acquisitions market is experiencing a significant boom in 2026, largely driven by a surge in AI-related deals. However, this increased activity is also leading to tighter cash availability for transactions.

title: Expert Take: Merger Control Beyond 2025: Preparing for the 2026 Landscape - ETLegalWorld.com
summary: Experts are advising companies to prepare for the evolving merger control landscape in 2026, following developments beyond 2025. This involves understanding new regulatory challenges and compliance requirements for future M&A activities.

title: 10 Major Mergers and Acquisitions of 2025 - AlphaSense
summary: AlphaSense has compiled a list of the ten most significant mergers and acquisitions that occurred throughout 2025. This review highlights key transactions that shaped the corporate landscape in the previous year.

title: Netflix to Buy Warner Bros. and HBO Max in $82.7 Billion Deal - Variety
summary: Netflix is set to acquire Warner Bros. and HBO Max in a massive deal valued at $82.7 billion. This acquisition will significantly reshape the media and entertainment industry, consolidating major streaming and content assets.
